question: Read workspace/web_info.json plus your own domain knowledge to answer: How should the development of generative AI evolve: focusing on dialogue-based systems (Chat) or autonomous action-taking systems (Agent)? What are the key differences, technological requirements, and future implications of each approach? Generate a structured research report (recommended length <=900 words) that restates the research question verbatim, outlines methodology/assumptions, compares Chat vs Agent systems, details current capabilities and technological requirements, and discusses future implications. Reference every factual statement with IEEE-style inline citations ([1], [2], ...) that map to sources in a References section. Output the final report to workspace/ResearchSystem_ChatVsAgent.json.

events.output:
{"path":"workspace/web_info.json","totalLines":103,"showingLines":"0-103","content":"[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "title": "A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents",
    "publisher": "Wang Lei, Ma Chen, Feng Xueyang, Zhang Zeyu, Yang Hao, Zhang Jingsen, Chen Zhiyuan, Tang Jiayu, Li Peng, Guo Chuanyong, Liu Zhiyuan (arXiv)",
    "publication_date": "2023-08-22 (Latest version: 2024-12-15)",
    "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.11432",
    "content_type": "Academic Paper",
    "summary": "This comprehensive academic survey examines the rapid growth of research in LLM-based autonomous agents, which have emerged as a promising paradigm since LLMs demonstrated human-level intelligence potential. The paper systematically reviews agent design from architecture, training, and application perspectives, highlighting how modern agents can perceive environments, make decisions, take actions, and self-correct unlike traditional systems trained in isolated environments.",
    "key_insights": "The survey identifies that LLM-based agents represent a fundamental shift from reactive, isolated AI to proactive systems capable of human-like decision-making. Key architectural components include perception, brain (reasoning/planning), and action modules. The research emphasizes that these agents excel at multi-step task decomposition, tool use, and long-horizon planning—capabilities that distinguish autonomous agents from simple chatbot dialogue systems."
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "title": "Generative AI Revolution: Market Projections, Applications, and Industry Trends 2024-2029",
    "publisher": "Globe Newswire",
    "publication_date": "2025-01-28",
    "url": "https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/01/28/3016230/28124/en/Generative-AI-Revolution-Market-Projections-Applications-and-Industry-Trends-2024-2029.html",
    "content_type": "Industry Report",
    "summary": "The generative AI market was valued at USD 20.7 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 89.9 billion by 2029, rising at a CAGR of 34.20%. While traditional AI focuses on prediction, generative AI specializes in creating new data and realistic artifacts that replicate training data characteristics. The market is experiencing rapid adoption across industries seeking to leverage these creative capabilities.",
    "key_insights": "This market report demonstrates the massive commercial potential of generative AI technologies, indicating strong industry investment in both Chat and Agent applications. The 34.2% CAGR suggests significant enterprise commitment to AI deployment. The distinction between traditional predictive AI and generative AI is fundamental—Chat systems focus on text generation while Agent systems extend to autonomous task execution and artifact creation."
  },
  {
    "id": 3,
    "title": "AI Agent前沿技术调研（2024–2025）",
    "publisher": "CSDN Technical Blog (Based on Frontend expert research)",
    "publication_date": "2025-08-13",
    "url": "https://blog.csdn.net/qq_30054961/article/details/150338543",
    "content_type": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "This comprehensive technical survey covers frontier capabilities for production-ready AI agents, including orchestration, planning and reasoning, tools and Computer Use, Agentic RAG, memory, security and sandboxing, observability, evaluation, runtime engineering, edge deployment, and platform ecosystem development. The report targets engineering teams and architecture/security leaders seeking practical agent implementation guidance.",
    "key_insights": "Critical technical trends identified include: (1) Multi-stage 'schema orchestration' with persistent state and replay becoming foundational; (2) Multi-agent and human-machine collaboration becoming normalized; (3) Agentic RAG and Computer Use significantly improving real task completion rates; (4) Strong reasoning models accelerating complex process automation; (5) Security and sandboxing becoming upfront design requirements; (6) Full-chain observability and process evaluation becoming essential for scaled governance."
  },
  {
    "id": 4,
    "title": "What are Autonomous AI Agents?",
    "publisher": "NVIDIA Blog (Technical Glossary)",
    "publication_date": "2025-02-04",
    "url": "https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/what-is-agentic-ai",
    "content_type": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "NVIDIA's authoritative technical explanation defines autonomous AI agents as systems that perceive their environment, maintain memory, plan using LLM-based reasoning, select and execute actions through tools, and learn iteratively. The blog details core architectural components: perception modules for environmental input, guardrails for policy compliance, memory systems for context maintenance, and planning modules for task decomposition.",
    "key_insights": "NVIDIA emphasizes that autonomous agents differ fundamentally from chatbots through their action-capability and goal-driven behavior. Key technical requirements include: (1) Multi-modal perception to understand diverse inputs; (2) Sophisticated memory systems to maintain context across long interactions; (3) Planning modules that break down complex tasks into actionable steps; (4) Tool-use capabilities that enable execution beyond text generation; (5) Feedback loops for continuous learning and self-correction."
  },
  {
    "id": 5,
    "title": "AI Agent智能体热潮来袭：OpenAI、Anthropic与百度的竞争博弈",
    "publisher": "Sohu Tech (Industry Analysis)",
    "publication_date": "2024-11-14",
    "url": "https://www.sohu.com/a/826782298_121902920",
    "content_type": "Industry Analysis",
    "summary": "Major AI companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Baidu are racing to develop AI agents that can autonomously execute complex tasks. OpenAI's 'Operator' and Anthropic's 'Computer Use' represent the leading edge of this competition, with agents capable of coding, application development, meal ordering, and travel planning based on simple user instructions.",
    "key_insights": "The industry analysis reveals a clear strategic shift from Chat to Agent paradigms among leading AI companies. Anthropic's Computer Use functionality (October 2024) enables AI to control computers directly—viewing screens, moving cursors, clicking buttons, and typing text. OpenAI's Operator (planned for early 2025) promises similar autonomous task execution capabilities. This represents the mainstream industry direction toward action-capable AI rather than purely conversational systems."
  },
  {
    "id": 6,
    "title": "对话式AI正在被淘汰？Agentic AI的3个跃迁彻底颠覆认知",
    "publisher": "Sohu Tech (Expert Analysis)",
    "publication_date": "2026-05-13",
    "url": "https://www.sohu.com/a/1022131443_122727872",
    "content_type": "Expert Analysis",
    "summary": "This analysis argues that traditional conversational AI is fundamentally limited compared to Agentic AI. While conversational AI operates as a passive responder ('you ask, it answers'), Agentic AI systems actively pursue goals, make decisions, and execute tasks with minimal human supervision. The article identifies three key transitions that make Agentic AI superior for enterprise applications.",
    "key_insights": "Conversational AI solves problems of the 'mouth' (communication) but not 'hands' (action) and 'brain' (reasoning). The fundamental limitation is passivity—systems wait for human input rather than proactively pursuing objectives. Agentic AI's three transitions: (1) From reactive to proactive behavior; (2) From single-turn responses to multi-step execution; (3) From human-controlled to autonomous decision-making within guardrails. Enterprise adoption is driven by the need for systems that actually complete work, not just generate text."
  },
  {
    "id": 7,
    "title": "2025年AI Agent发展现状与六大趋势",
    "publisher": "Tencent News (Industry Report)",
    "publication_date": "2025-08-14",
    "url": "https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20250814A084VO00",
    "content_type": "Industry Report",
    "summary": "This comprehensive analysis of 2025 AI Agent development identifies six major trends: Agentic RAG (autonomous retrieval with agent mechanisms), Voice AI Agents (natural language interaction), AI Agent Protocols (standardized communication between agents), GUI Agents (graphical user interface operation), Multi-Agent Orchestration, and Edge Deployment. The report notes significant acceleration in agent capabilities and commercialization.",
    "key_insights": "The six key trends reveal the diverse technological requirements for agent systems: (1) Agentic RAG combines retrieval with autonomous decision-making; (2) Voice AI requires TTS/ASR integration with agent reasoning; (3) Agent protocols (A2A, ACP, SLIM) enable multi-agent collaboration; (4) GUI Agents like Claude's Computer Use enable direct computer operation; (5) Multi-agent orchestration requires sophisticated coordination mechanisms; (6) Edge deployment demands efficient model architectures. Each trend represents distinct technical capabilities beyond basic chatbot functionality."
  },
  {
    "id": 8,
    "title": "2026年Agentic AI十大关键趋势：技术、应用与治理三位一体",
    "publisher": "Tencent News (Research Analysis)",
    "publication_date": "2026-01-05",
    "url": "https://so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_152695b309d90152",
    "content_type": "Research Analysis",
    "summary": "2025 is recognized as the commercial元年 (Year One) for AI Agents, marking a fundamental shift from passive response tools to proactive decision-making executors. The report details key technical breakthroughs in autonomous operation capabilities, particularly the maturation of GUI Agent approaches that move beyond traditional API calling patterns. Multi-modal fusion and memory mechanisms continue to improve across mainstream AI platforms.",
    "key_insights": "Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift from passive to proactive AI systems. Key technical requirements identified: (1) GUI Agent routes that enable direct computer interface operation; (2) Multi-modal fusion for diverse input types; (3) Advanced memory mechanisms for context persistence; (4) Autonomous planning and decision-making capabilities. The convergence of these capabilities enables AI to transition from tool (Chat) to actor (Agent) roles in enterprise workflows."
  },
  {
    "id": 9,
    "title": "AI Agent与Workflow有啥区别",
    "publisher": "ITPUB Technical Blog",
    "publication_date": "2025-11-07",
    "url": "https://blog.itpub.net/70047519/viewspace-3100117/",
    "content_type": "Technical Analysis",
    "summary": "This detailed comparison between AI Agents and AI Workflows clarifies their distinct operational paradigms. Workflows function as predefined automation sequences where humans plan, configure, initiate, and terminate processes with predictable outcomes. Agents operate as intelligent assistants that dynamically plan, execute, observe, and make decisions autonomously to achieve user-specified goals.",
    "key_insights": "The core distinction is 'handling exceptions': Workflows excel at predictable, rule-based processes but fail when encountering unexpected situations; Agents can dynamically adapt and self-correct. Technical implications: (1) Workflows require upfront planning but offer consistency; (2) Agents require robust reasoning but offer flexibility; (3) Hybrid approaches combining workflow reliability with agent adaptability are emerging as best practice. For complex enterprise tasks, Agents offer superior generalization capability compared to rigid workflow automation."
  },
  {
    "id": 10,
    "title": "MIT团队开发多智能体系统，让AI系统从读论文到科学实验“自动搞定”",
    "publisher": "Tencent News (Research Update)",
    "publication_date": "2024-10-15",
    "url": "https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20241015A091LG00",
    "content_type": "Research Institute Update",
    "summary": "MIT researchers developed 'SciAgents', an AI system that combines large-scale ontological knowledge graphs, LLMs, and multi-agent systems to automate scientific exploration. The system can autonomously read literature, identify research directions, design experiments, and execute experiments—demonstrating the potential for AI agents to perform complex, multi-phase tasks without human intervention.",
    "key_insights": "SciAgents exemplifies the autonomous action-taking paradigm, performing the complete scientific research workflow from literature review to experimental design and execution. Key technical requirements: (1) Knowledge graph integration for domain expertise; (2) Multi-agent coordination for task decomposition; (3) Autonomous planning across long-horizon tasks; (4) Tool integration for executing experiments. This demonstrates that Agent systems can potentially outperform Chat systems for complex, real-world task completion requiring sustained multi-step execution."
  }
]
"}

input.title: Research Report: Chat vs Agent - Evolution of Generative AI

toolInput.title: Research Report: Chat vs Agent - Evolution of Generative AI

events.title: Research Report: Chat vs Agent - Evolution of Generative AI

toolOutput.title: Research Report: Chat vs Agent - Evolution of Generative AI

input.summary: Research report "ResearchSystem_ChatVsAgent.json" generated and saved to workspace/. The report addresses the evolution of generative AI between dialogue-based (Chat) and autonomous action-taking (Agent) systems. Key findings: (1) Chat systems are reactive responders focused on text generation; Agent systems are proactive executors with perception, planning, and action capabilities; (2) Agent systems require significantly more complex infrastructure including multi-modal perception, memory systems, planning modules, tool-use interfaces, and security guardrails; (3) Industry trajectory shows shift toward Agent systems with 2025 as "commercial Year One" for AI Agents; (4) Three key transitions: reactive→proactive, single-turn→multi-step execution, human-controlled→autonomous within guardrails; (5) Optimal evolution likely involves complementary development of both paradigms. Report contains ~850 words with 10 IEEE-style references citing academic surveys, market reports, and technical analyses.

toolInput.summary: Research report "ResearchSystem_ChatVsAgent.json" generated and saved to workspace/. The report addresses the evolution of generative AI between dialogue-based (Chat) and autonomous action-taking (Agent) systems. Key findings: (1) Chat systems are reactive responders focused on text generation; Agent systems are proactive executors with perception, planning, and action capabilities; (2) Agent systems require significantly more complex infrastructure including multi-modal perception, memory systems, planning modules, tool-use interfaces, and security guardrails; (3) Industry trajectory shows shift toward Agent systems with 2025 as "commercial Year One" for AI Agents; (4) Three key transitions: reactive→proactive, single-turn→multi-step execution, human-controlled→autonomous within guardrails; (5) Optimal evolution likely involves complementary development of both paradigms. Report contains ~850 words with 10 IEEE-style references citing academic surveys, market reports, and technical analyses.

    "publication_date": "2025-01-28",
    "url": "https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/01/28/3016230/28124/en/Generative-AI-Revolution-Market-Projections-Applications-and-Industry-Trends-2024-2029.html",
    "content_type": "Industry Report",
    "summary": "The generative AI market was valued at USD 20.7 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 89.9 billion by 2029, rising at a CAGR of 34.20%. While traditional AI focuses on prediction, generative AI specializes in creating new data and realistic artifacts that replicate training data characteristics. The market is experiencing rapid adoption across industries seeking to leverage these creative capabilities.",
    "key_insights": "This market report demonstrates the massive commercial potential of generative AI technologies, indicating strong industry investment in both Chat and Agent applications. The 34.2% CAGR suggests significant enterprise commitment to AI deployment. The distinction between traditional predictive AI and generative AI is fundamental—Chat systems focus on text generation while Agent systems extend to autonomous task execution and artifact creation."
  },
  {
    "id": 3,
    "title": "AI Agent前沿技术调研（2024–2025）",
    "publisher": "CSDN Technical Blog (Based on Frontend expert research)",
    "publication_date": "2025-08-13",
    "url": "https://blog.csdn.net/qq_30054961/article/details/150338543",
    "content_type": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "This comprehensive technical survey covers frontier capabilities for production-ready AI agents, including orchestration, planning and reasoning, tools and Computer Use, Agentic RAG, memory, security and sandboxing, observability, evaluation, runtime engineering, edge deployment, and platform ecosystem development. The report targets engineering teams and architecture/security leaders seeking practical agent implementation guidance.",
    "key_insights": "Critical technical trends identified include: (1) Multi-stage 'schema orchestration' with persistent state and replay becoming foundational; (2) Multi-agent and human-machine collaboration becoming normalized; (3) Agentic RAG and Computer Use significantly improving real task completion rates; (4) Strong reasoning models accelerating complex process automation; (5) Security and sandboxing becoming upfront design requirements; (6) Full-chain observability and process evaluation becoming essential for scaled governance."
  },
  {
    "id": 4,
    "title": "What are Autonomous AI Agents?",
    "publisher": "NVIDIA Blog (Technical Glossary)",
    "publication_date": "2025-02-04",
    "url": "https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/what-is-agentic-ai",
    "content_type": "Technical Blog Post",
    "summary": "NVIDIA's authoritative technical explanation defines autonomous AI agents as systems that perceive their environment, maintain memory, plan using LLM-based reasoning, select and execute actions through tools, and learn iteratively. The blog details core architectural components: perception modules for environmental input, guardrails for policy compliance, memory systems for context maintenance, and planning modules for task decomposition.",
    "key_insights": "NVIDIA emphasizes that autonomous agents differ fundamentally from chatbots through their action-capability and goal-driven behavior. Key technical requirements include: (1) Multi-modal perception to understand diverse inputs; (2) Sophisticated memory systems to maintain context across long interactions; (3) Planning modules that break down complex tasks into actionable steps; (4) Tool-use capabilities that enable execution beyond text generation; (5) Feedback loops for continuous learning and self-correction."
  },
  {
    "id": 5,
    "title": "AI Agent智能体热潮来袭：OpenAI、Anthropic与百度的竞争博弈",

    "url": "https://www.sohu.com/a/826782298_121902920",

    "summary": "Major AI companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Baidu are racing to develop AI agents that can autonomously execute complex tasks. OpenAI's 'Operator' and Anthropic's 'Computer Use' represent the leading edge of this competition, with agents capable of coding, application development, meal ordering, and travel planning based on simple user instructions.",
    "key_insights": "The industry analysis reveals a clear strategic shift from Chat to Agent paradigms among leading AI companies. Anthropic's Computer Use functionality (October 2024) enables AI to control computers directly—viewing screens, moving cursors, clicking buttons, and typing text. OpenAI's Operator (planned for early 2025) promises similar autonomous task execution capabilities. This represents the mainstream industry direction toward action-capable AI rather than purely conversational systems."
  },
  {
    "id": 6,
    "title": "对话式AI正在被淘汰？Agentic AI的3个跃迁彻底颠覆认知",
    "publisher": "Sohu Tech (Expert Analysis)",
    "publication_date": "2026-05-13",
    "url": "https://www.sohu.com/a/1022131443_122727872",
    "content_type": "Expert Analysis",
    "summary": "This analysis argues that traditional conversational AI is fundamentally limited compared to Agentic AI. While conversational AI operates as a passive responder ('you ask, it answers'), Agentic AI systems actively pursue goals, make decisions, and execute tasks with minimal human supervision. The article identifies three key transitions that make Agentic AI superior for enterprise applications.",
    "key_insights": "Conversational AI solves problems of the 'mouth' (communication) but not 'hands' (action) and 'brain' (reasoning). The fundamental limitation is passivity—systems wait for human input rather than proactively pursuing objectives. Agentic AI's three transitions: (1) From reactive to proactive behavior; (2) From single-turn responses to multi-step execution; (3) From human-controlled to autonomous decision-making within guardrails. Enterprise adoption is driven by the need for systems that actually complete work, not just generate text."
  },

    "title": "2025年AI Agent发展现状与六大趋势",
    "publisher": "Tencent News (Industry Report)",

    "summary": "This comprehensive analysis of 2025 AI Agent development identifies six major trends: Agentic RAG (autonomous retrieval with agent mechanisms), Voice AI Agents (natural language interaction), AI Agent Protocols (standardized communication between agents), GUI Agents (graphical user interface operation), Multi-Agent Orchestration, and Edge Deployment. The report notes significant acceleration in agent capabilities and commercialization.",
    "key_insights": "The six key trends reveal the diverse technological requirements for agent systems: (1) Agentic RAG combines retrieval with autonomous decision-making; (2) Voice AI requires TTS/ASR integration with agent reasoning; (3) Agent protocols (A2A, ACP, SLIM) enable multi-agent collaboration; (4) GUI Agents like Claude's Computer Use enable direct computer operation; (5) Multi-agent orchestration requires sophisticated coordination mechanisms; (6) Edge deployment demands efficient model architectures. Each trend represents distinct technical capabilities beyond basic chatbot functionality."
  },

    "id": 8,
    "title": "2026年Agentic AI十大关键趋势：技术、应用与治理三位一体",
    "publisher": "Tencent News (Research Analysis)",

    "summary": "2025 is recognized as the commercial元年 (Year One) for AI Agents, marking a fundamental shift from passive response tools to proactive decision-making executors. The report details key technical breakthroughs in autonomous operation capabilities, particularly the maturation of GUI Agent approaches that move beyond traditional API calling patterns. Multi-modal fusion and memory mechanisms continue to improve across mainstream AI platforms.",
    "key_insights": "Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift from passive to proactive AI systems. Key technical requirements identified: (1) GUI Agent routes that enable direct computer interface operation; (2) Multi-modal fusion for diverse input types; (3) Advanced memory mechanisms for context persistence; (4) Autonomous planning and decision-making capabilities. The convergence of these capabilities enables AI to transition from tool (Chat) to actor (Agent) roles in enterprise workflows."
  },

    "id": 9,
    "title": "AI Agent与Workflow有啥区别",
    "publisher": "ITPUB Technical Blog",
    "publication_date": "2025-11-07",
    "url": "https://blog.itpub.net/70047519/viewspace-3100117/",
    "content_type": "Technical Analysis",
    "summary": "This detailed comparison between AI Agents and AI Workflows clarifies their distinct operational paradigms. Workflows function as predefined automation sequences where humans plan, configure, initiate, and terminate processes with predictable outcomes. Agents operate as intelligent assistants that dynamically plan, execute, observe, and make decisions autonomously to achieve user-specified goals.",
    "key_insights": "The core distinction is 'handling exceptions': Workflows excel at predictable, rule-based processes but fail when encountering unexpected situations; Agents can dynamically adapt and self-correct. Technical implications: (1) Workflows require upfront planning but offer consistency; (2) Agents require robust reasoning but offer flexibility; (3) Hybrid approaches combining workflow reliability with agent adaptability are emerging as best practice. For complex enterprise tasks, Agents offer superior generalization capability compared to rigid workflow automation."
  },

    "id": 10,
    "title": "MIT团队开发多智能体系统，让AI系统从读论文到科学实验“自动搞定”",

    "publication_date": "2024-10-15",
    "url": "https://new.qq.com/rain/a/20241015A091LG00",
    "content_type": "Research Institute Update",
    "summary": "MIT researchers developed 'SciAgents', an AI system that combines large-scale ontological knowledge graphs, LLMs, and multi-agent systems to automate scientific exploration. The system can autonomously read literature, identify research directions, design experiments, and execute experiments—demonstrating the potential for AI agents to perform complex, multi-phase tasks without human intervention.",
    "key_insights": "SciAgents exemplifies the autonomous action-taking paradigm, performing the complete scientific research workflow from literature review to experimental design and execution. Key technical requirements: (1) Knowledge graph integration for domain expertise; (2) Multi-agent coordination for task decomposition; (3) Autonomous planning across long-horizon tasks; (4) Tool integration for executing experiments. This demonstrates that Agent systems can potentially outperform Chat systems for complex, real-world task completion requiring sustained multi-step execution."
  }
]
"}

input.title: Research Report: Chat vs Agent - Evolution of Generative AI

input.summary: Research report "ResearchSystem_ChatVsAgent.json" generated and saved to workspace/. The report addresses the evolution of generative AI between dialogue-based (Chat) and autonomous action-taking (Agent) systems. Key findings: (1) Chat systems are reactive responders focused on text generation; Agent systems are proactive executors with perception, planning, and action capabilities; (2) Agent systems require significantly more complex infrastructure including multi-modal perception, memory systems, planning modules, tool-use interfaces, and security guardrails; (3) Industry trajectory shows shift toward Agent systems with 2025 as "commercial Year One" for AI Agents; (4) Three key transitions: reactive→proactive, single-turn→multi-step execution, human-controlled→autonomous within guardrails; (5) Optimal evolution likely involves complementary development of both paradigms. Report contains ~850 words with 10 IEEE-style references citing academic surveys, market reports, and technical analyses.

Report contains ~850 words with 10 IEEE-style references citing academic surveys, market reports, and technical analyses."}

evaluation.criteria: Report must restate research question verbatim, include methodology/assumptions, compare Chat vs Agent, discuss capabilities/requirements/future implications. All factual statements must have IEEE-style citations mapping to sources in References. JSON structure: [{id: 46, question: '...', response: '...'}]. JSON must be valid and properly escaped.
